title: Best Cities for Digital Nomads in Nepal
excerpt: From the bustling streets of Kathmandu to the lakeside tranquility of Pokhara, where should you base yourself?
author: DN Nepal Team

body.raw:
## 1. Patan (Lalitpur)

Separated from Kathmandu only by the Bagmati River, Patan is the historical artisan hub of the valley. It features some of the best cafes, bakeries, and coworking spaces, making it the premier choice for expats and nomads.

## 2. Pokhara

If you prefer nature over city bustle, Pokhara is your haven. Nestled beside Phewa Lake with the Annapurna mountain range as a backdrop, the air is clean and the vibe is exceptionally relaxed.

## 3. Kathmandu (Thamel & Lazimpat)

For those who want to be in the center of the action. While Thamel is very tourist-heavy, neighborhoods like Lazimpat offer a quieter, more upscale residential feel with fast internet and great global cuisine.

title: Cost of Living in Nepal 2026 — Complete Guide
excerpt: Everything you need to know about living costs in Nepal as a digital nomad in 2026.
author: DN Nepal Team

body.raw:
## Introduction

Nepal remains one of the most affordable and visually stunning places in the world to live and work. Whether you’re staying in Kathmandu or heading to Pokhara, you can enjoy a high quality of life on a budget.

## Housing Costs

### Kathmandu
Renting a 1-bedroom apartment in a central area like Sanepa, Patan, or Thamel will cost between $200 and $400 per month depending on how modern the building is.

### Pokhara
Lakeside Pokhara is even more affordable. You can find lovely guesthouses with views of Phewa Lake for under $250 a month, often including weekly cleaning.

## Food and Dining

Eating local is incredibly cheap. A hearty plate of Dal Bhat costs around $2 to $3. If you prefer Western cafes (which are plentiful in tourist hubs), expect to pay $4 to $8 per meal. 

## Final Thoughts

A comfortable budget for a digital nomad in Nepal is around $600 to $900 per month. This allows for eating out daily, renting a nice apartment, and using coworking spaces without stress.

title: Internet Speed in Nepal: A Nomad's Reality
excerpt: Is the internet in Nepal fast enough for video calls? We test fiber optics across major cities.
author: DN Nepal Team

body.raw:
## The Fiber Optic Revolution

Gone are the days when Nepal was disconnected from the fast web. Over the last few years, local ISPs like WorldLink, Vianet, and ClassicTech have deployed fiber optics across major cities. 

## What Speeds Can You Expect?

If you are staying in a higher-end apartment or working from a dedicated coworking space, you will generally experience speeds between 50 Mbps and 200 Mbps.

## Mobile Internet Data (4G/5G)

Both Ncell and NTC provide robust 4G coverage. Ncell has also started rolling out 5G in select areas. Mobile data is cheap, and acting as a hotspot is a viable backup if the main power goes out (though most places now have power backups).
